response.justification: Bogdan has a broad polyglot background and genuine geospatial domain interest demonstrated through GISCollective and Green Map work, but the role's core requirements are largely unmet. His C++ experience is limited to a brief National Instruments engagement in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — no modern C++ in commercial production and no Rust whatsoever. His primary commercial stack is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack. While he has meaningful distributed-systems exposure from Dunnhumby's high-throughput microservices work, there is no evidence of routing tile pipeline design, navigation guidance systems, or real-time traffic data integration, which are central to this role.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience across GISCollective, Green Map Association, and a FOSS4G conference talk on open GIS tooling
Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with emphasis on low-latency async I/O and high-performance data handling
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a broad polyglot history demonstrating willingness to adopt new technologies
concerns: C++ is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; no modern C++ in commercial production and zero Rust experience
Primary commercial expertise is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, which is fundamentally misaligned with the C++ routing pipeline focus of this role
Geospatial work is centered on data collection and visualization (GIS platforms), not routing tile generation, pipeline latency optimization, or navigation accuracy systems
